summary: Large Purin/Jigglypuff SpecialN Rollout decompilation pass. The PR filled many ftPr_SpecialN.c TODO stubs across Enter, Anim, IASA, Phys, Coll, and callback helpers, expanded Purin motion/attribute structs, and retyped init data used for rollout scaling and collision. decomp.dev reported +9540 matched bytes and 27 new 100% matches, with several additional near-matches; it also flagged 3 broken ftKirby matches, so cross-unit match fallout remained a risk.

key_files.role: Main rollout decomp pass; +892/-55 lines implementing many SpecialN ground/air state callbacks, helper inlines, physics, collision, IASA, hit/end logic, and mirror callback behavior.
Expanded ftPurin_MotionVars.specialn fields and ftPurinAttributes offsets through xD8 so rollout code could use typed timers, speeds, thresholds, and scale/damage parameters.
Retyped rollout-related init data: Vec4 constants became f32[4] arrays, and a Vec3 pair became an ftCollisionBox.
Exported ftPr_Init_803D05C8, ftPr_Init_803D05D8, and ftPr_Init_803D0610 with their new types; added ftCommon forward include.
Updated ftPr_SpecialS_8013DD54 prototype to take a bool argument used by normal vs turn SFX/angle logic.

what_changed: Implemented callback/setup helpers including setupPurinCallbacks, normalizeAndSetRollAngle, playRollSFX, scaleAnimStep, hitCapsuleToggle, ftPr_SpecialS_8013D8E4, ftPr_SpecialS_8013DA24, ftPr_SpecialS_8013DC64, and ftPr_SpecialS_8013DD54.
Implemented ftPr_SpecialAirN_Enter and many rollout animation callbacks: loop/full/release/turn/end, air charge loop/full/start-turn/end, and hit animation logic.
Implemented IASA transitions for charge release and turn behavior, using held-input checks, left-stick threshold checks, motion-state changes, callback reset, SFX, and velocity setup.

Implemented or substantially improved rollout physics for ground release, ground turn, air charge release, and air start turn, including speed clamping, slope/floor influence, deceleration, fall handling, and charge decay.

Implemented several ground/air collision transitions, including SpecialN loop/full air transitions, air-to-ground start/charge transitions, turn collisions using ftCollisionBox, hit collision landing/fall-special handling, and ftPr_SpecialN_8014222C mirror logic.
Changed ftPr_Init_803D05C8 and ftPr_Init_803D05D8 from Vec4 to f32[4] because rollout scale animation indexes them by frame.
Changed ftPr_Init_803D0610 from a Vec3 array-style initializer to ftCollisionBox and exported it for collision helpers such as ft_80082888, ft_80082978, and ft_800824A0.
Expanded Purin struct placeholders rather than fully naming semantics: specialn gained typed x0/x4/x8/xC/x10/x14/x18/x24/x28/x2C/x30 fields, and attributes gained many x4C through xD8 offsets.

decomp.dev reported 27 newly matched functions in main/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialN and 7 high-percent unmatched improvements, including ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Anim at 99.77%, ftPr_SpecialNTurn_Anim at 98.59%, and several Phys functions in the 93-98% range.
smart_moves: Factored repeated exact-codegen-sensitive behavior into small static inline helpers for callback setup, angle normalization, scale stepping, hit-capsule toggling, and rollout SFX selection.
Retyped data symbols to match actual usage instead of forcing previous convenient types: f32[4] arrays for indexed scale multipliers and ftCollisionBox for collision routines.
Kept unknown struct fields as offset-style xNN names while still replacing opaque padding with correctly sized typed fields needed by the decomp.
Used explicit MotionFlags bitmasks as local static constants with TODO notes rather than inventing uncertain flag names.
Preserved stack/codegen details with PAD_STACK and dummy u8 arrays, which is important for matching these fighter callbacks.
Did not over-simplify mirrored ground/air and slope/velocity branches; the code keeps repetitive absolute-value and sign logic that likely reflects the original compiler output.
Author explicitly documented that some functions were close enough rather than 100% matches, making the near-match tradeoff visible instead of hiding it.
decomp_lessons: Fighter special-move files often require decomping the whole state-machine family together: Enter, Anim, IASA, Phys, Coll, hit/end callbacks, and mirror callbacks all share motion vars and callback setup.
Header/data typing can unlock source matches: callers that index constants or pass collision extents need exact data types exposed in init headers.
When semantics are not yet certain, offset-based names like xA0 or x2C are preferable to misleading guesses; add typed fields only where the diff proves size and usage.
Centralize repeated state restoration such as fighter callbacks and part rotations, but keep helper functions inline when matching depends on call structure.
decomp.dev match reports are part of the evidence: this PR made large progress but also showed unrelated ftKirby match breakage, so future struct/header changes should check cross-unit regressions.
Near-matches can still be valuable when they produce many exact matches and document remaining gaps, but they should leave clear follow-up targets.
naming_conventions: Public Purin state callbacks follow ftPr_SpecialN<State>_<Phase> patterns such as ftPr_SpecialNLoop_Anim, ftPr_SpecialNRelease_IASA, ftPr_SpecialNTurn_Phys, and ftPr_SpecialNHit_Coll.
Air variants use the SpecialAirN prefix, for example ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeLoop_Anim and ftPr_SpecialAirNStartTurn_Coll.
Unknown or legacy helper symbols kept address-suffixed names such as ftPr_SpecialS_8013D8E4, ftPr_SpecialS_8013DA24, ftPr_SpecialS_8013DC64, and ftPr_SpecialN_8014222C.
Static inline helpers used descriptive lowerCamelCase names: setupPurinCallbacks, normalizeAndSetRollAngle, playRollSFX, scaleAnimStep, and hitCapsuleToggle.
Purin motion vars and attributes retained offset names like x14, x2C, xA4, xC0, and xD8 until semantics are proven.
Init data symbols kept address-based names such as ftPr_Init_803D05C8, ftPr_Init_803D05D8, and ftPr_Init_803D0610, with offset comments in headers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8), PAD_STACK(16), PAD_STACK(4 * 4), and local dummy u8 arrays to reproduce stack layout.
Used static u32 const motion-flag masks near each state transition instead of global symbolic names, preserving bit patterns while marking them TODO.
Used exact float/math forms including M_PI, M_PI_2, deg_to_rad, atan2f, and casts like (f32) (0.2 * da->x6C * ...).
Introduced NEGABS(x) as a local absolute-value macro and repeated manual sign checks in places where simplification might change codegen.
Kept angle normalization as while loops around 0 and M_PI * 2, then applied ftPartSetRotX to FtPart_YRotN and ftPartSetRotY to FtPart_TopN.
Indexed ftPr_Init_803D05C8 and ftPr_Init_803D05D8 by mv.pr.specialn.x8 to reproduce the four-frame JObj scale animation.
Used ftCollisionBox ftPr_Init_803D0610 with ft_80082888, ft_80082978, and ft_800824A0 for rollout ground/air collision checks.
Matched mirror behavior in ftPr_SpecialN_8014222C by negating self_vel.x, gr_vel, ground accel, rollout motion fields, facing_dir, and cached angle/facing vector components.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the slice.
Author note: "I prefer to only push 100% matches, but some of these felt close enough to be worth it."
decomp.dev bot reported matched code 58.24% (+0.25%, +9540 bytes) and 27 new 100% matches, all listed under main/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialN.
decomp.dev bot also reported 7 improvements in unmatched items, including several near-matched rollout Anim/Phys functions and .sdata2.
decomp.dev bot flagged 3 broken ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialN matches: ftKb_SpecialAirS_Enter, ftKb_SpecialHi1_Anim, and ftKb_EatJump1_Anim.
follow_up_queries: ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Anim remaining TODO or near-match status
